entry.title: Paper published in EMIR, Bacterial community composition and predicted function in surf zone seawater
entry.content:
Article title

Li, Y., Hablützel, P.I., Liu, Z., Van Acker, E., Janssen, C.R., Asselman, J. and De Rijcke, M., 2024. Seasonal dynamics of bacterial community structure and function in the surf zone seawater of a recreational beach in Ostend, Belgium. Environmental Microbiology Reports, 16(6), p.e70031. https://doi.org/10.1111/1758-2229.70031

Submission timeline

DateStatus
2024-11-08 FrOnline
2024-10-08 TuAccepted
2024-08-14 WeSubmitted revised version
2024-07-30 TuMajor revision
2024-06-24 Tuunder Review
2024-06-20 TuSelected the transfer option in the submission system after discussing with Maarten.
2024-06-08 SaRejected due to a mismatch with the journal’s scope. The editors recommended resubmission to Environmental microbiology reports, IF: 3.6 (2023-2024).
2024-06-05Under review: This submission is under consideration and cannot be edited. Further information will be emailed to you by the journal editorial office.
2024-06-04Resubmitted. 1.I removed the figures and tables from the manuscript main text; the figures are now provided in a separate DOC file. 2.Carolien （VLIZ） said the files were too large to publish as an IMIS dataset, so we could only create a dataset from a subset of the data and note that the full raw data are available in the MDA upon request—just contact the data team. Done, and we replaced the previous DOI in the resubmitted manuscript.
2024-06-02Submission was returned for resubmission because 1.the figures were too large for the system to generate a draft and the figures must be uploaded separately, and 2.The dataset DOI is invalid.
2024-05-31Submitted to Environmental Microbiology, IF: 5.1 (2023-2024).

entry.title: Paper published in STOTEN, The immunostimulatory activity of sea spray aerosols
entry.content:
Article title

Li, Y., Schütte, W., Dekeukeleire, M., Janssen, C., Boon, N., Asselman, J., Lebeer, S., Spacova, I. and De Rijcke, M., 2024. The immunostimulatory activity of sea spray aerosols: bacteria and endotoxins activate TLR4, TLR2/6, NF-κB and IRF in human cells. Science of The Total Environment, p.171969. https://doi.org/10.1016/j.scitotenv.2024.171969

Related press release

20240522 by Gezondheid en wetenschap
Fact check: Study does not show that living by the sea is healthier
20240513 by Testerp
Bacteria in sea air strengthen immunity
20240513 by VRTNWS
Living by the sea is indeed healthier, research shows: “Our immune system is better trained there”

Submission timeline

DateStatus
2024-03-23-SaAccepted
2024-03-22-Fr1st revision: 3 - 3 - 3
2024-03-15-Fr1st revision: 3 - 2 - 2
2024-03-13-We1st revision: 3 - 1 - 1
2024-03-11-MoResubmitted, With Editor
2024-02-14-ThMajor Revision (three reviewers)
2024-02-07-WeRequired Reviews Complete: 12 - 3 - 2
2024-02-05-MoUnder Review: 12 - 3 - 1
2024-01-30-TuUnder Review: 12 - 3 - 0
2024-01-29-MoUnder Review: 12 - 2 - 0
2024-01-26-FrWith editor
2024-01-26-FrSubmission
